text:
Sure.

When Little Mouse woke up one peaceful morning, he wanted pancakes for breakfast. He dashed out of his cozy nest, leaving a trail of fluffy footprints behind him. He bounced along to the kitchen, humming an unlikely heroic tune. The pantry was empty; he wouldn’t find his favorite fruit plate today. Shrugging with disappointment, Little Mouse rummaged through a cabinet labeled "Bread."

After several fruitless searches, Little Mouse's ears twitched. Something was fluttering close. He peered out the window. A butterfly! One just like him. He darted outside and nuzzled the butterfly. In return, it gave him a sip of honey.

That afternoon, while swinging lazily beneath a tree, Little Mouse noticed movement between the leaves. A squirrel! It rattled a nut. Little Mouse lunged.

In a flurry of acrobatics barely matching his speed, he scooped up the nut. Heart pounding with exhilaration, he scampered back to
